claim_text: ding2020 (DISTS) is a representative deep-feature-distance metric used as a baseline in IQ comparisons.

sub_claims.text: DISTS (ding2020) is a deep-feature-distance image-quality metric (full-reference IQA built on CNN/VGG feature representations).

evidence.snippet: Our method is constructed by first nonlinearly transforming images to a multi-scale overcomplete representation, using a variant of the VGG convolutional neural network (CNN) [14].
show that the resulting Deep Image Structure and Texture Similarity (DISTS) index can be transformed into a proper metric in the mathematical sense.

Inspired by the form of SSIM [3], we defined separate quality measurements for the texture (using the global means) and the structure (using the global correlations) of each pair of corresponding feature maps

Using a convolutional neural network, we construct an injective and differentiable function that transforms images to multi-scale overcomplete representations... we describe an image quality method that combines correlations of these spatial averages ("texture similarity") with correlations of the feature maps ("structure similarity").

sub_claims.nuance: DISTS is explicitly built on VGG CNN feature maps and proven to be a proper distance metric, qualifying it unambiguously as a deep-feature-distance IQA metric.

sub_claims.text: DISTS is representative of/comparable to other deep-feature-based IQA baselines (e.g., LPIPS, DeepIQA, PieAPP) in standard IQ comparisons.

evidence.snippet: Popular IQA measures, including PSNR, SSIM [3], FSIM [11], VIF [4], GMSD [12], DeepIQA [13], PieAPP [8], and LPIPS [7], predict that image (b) has a better perceived quality than image (c)... In contrast, the proposed DISTS model makes the correct prediction.

We compared DISTS against a set of full-reference IQA methods, including nine knowledge-driven models and three data-driven CNN-based models... As LPIPS [7] has different configurations, we chose the default one (known as LPIPS-VGG-lin).
Results, reported in Table 1, demonstrate that DISTS performs favorably in comparison to both classic methods (e.g., PSNR and SSIM [3]) and CNN-based models (e.g., DeepIQA [13] and LPIPS [7]).

We also tested DISTS on BAPPS [7], a large-scale and highly-varied patch similarity dataset... showing that DISTS (which was not trained on BAPPS, or any similar database) achieves a comparable performance to LPIPS [7]

sub_claims.nuance: Within ding2020 itself DISTS is the proposed (not baseline) method, but it is positioned alongside the same family of deep-feature IQA metrics (LPIPS, DeepIQA, PieAPP) routinely used as baselines in downstream IQ comparisons; the manuscript's usage as a 'representative deep-feature-distance metric used as a baseline' is consistent with how ding2020 establishes its category.

phrasings_tried: DISTS is a deep-feature-based / CNN-feature-based full-reference image-quality metric
DISTS uses VGG feature representations to compute image quality (qualifies as a deep-feature-distance metric)
DISTS is a representative deep-feature IQA method commonly compared against / used as a baseline alongside LPIPS, PieAPP, DeepIQA in image-quality evaluations

section_checklist.section: Connections to Other Full-Reference IQA Methods
Applications to Texture Classification and Retrieval

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: ding2020 is the primary source for the DISTS metric — no indirect attribution; this is the originating publication.
attestation.out_of_context_check: In ding2020 itself DISTS is the proposed novel method, not a baseline; the manuscript's 'used as a baseline' framing reflects downstream usage in subsequent IQ work, not ding2020's own self-positioning. The category claim ('representative deep-feature-distance metric') matches ding2020 exactly. No context mismatch on the substantive category claim.
